items.name: Resonance Striker
items.description: A short haft ending in a tuning-fork head that rings on impact — favored in the Heights. The ring does the real damage, rattling bone and mechanism alike.

items.name: Prism Lens
items.description: A palm-sized cut crystal on a thong. Held to the eye it splits light into readings; held to the sun it can throw a focused beam. Plateau-made.
items.substrateNote: Cut Radiant crystal — a focus that gathers as well as splits.

items.name: Warden's Knife
items.description: A plain, beautifully-kept blade of green-black stone, the kind the Glade wardens carry. Cuts cord and defends a life; never used for cruelty without the grove somehow knowing.

items.name: Pass-Cloak
items.description: A heavy oiled cloak from the Roost, cut for the mountain trails — sheds rain, breaks wind, and hides a lot of a person on an exposed switchback.

items.name: Channel Pole
items.description: A marsh-pilot's pole from the Greywater — finds the bottom, moves a skiff, tests suspect ground, and cracks a skull in a pinch. Tam swears by his.

items.name: Greenlore Satchel
items.description: A herbalist's roll of pockets and vials — poultices, teas, antidotes, and the tools to make more from what the country gives.

items.name: Quiet Boots
items.description: Soft-soled, marsh-tanned boots that make no sound on natural ground and grip wet stone. The difference between being heard and not.

items.name: Storm-Glass
items.description: A sealed glass of shifting crystal that reads the coming weather a day out. Greta keeps one at the waystation; travelers covet them.

items.name: Healer's Draught
items.description: A bitter greenlore brew that closes wounds and steadies a failing body. One dose. Tastes like wet bark and works anyway.

items.name: Clarity Tea
items.description: A pale astringent tea that burns off exhaustion and sharpens a tired mind for a while. One dose; the crash comes later.

items.name: Smoke-Bead
items.description: A clay bead that bursts into a cloud of grey concealment when thrown. One use. Runners and cutpurses buy them by the string at the Roost.

items.name: Toll-Chits
items.description: A handful of the Kestrel's stamped brass chits — the Roost's own currency of favors owed. Spend them for passage, silence, or a door opened.

items.name: Choir-Shard
items.description: A sliver of stone from the Sunken Choir's drowned tiers that will not stop faintly ringing. It sharpens sonic work — and, held too long, starts to return voices. Not for the unwary.

evolution.note: The Choir-Shard changes the longer it's carried and used in sonic work — it needs no companion, only use and time. GM foreshadows the shift; the third stage carries a cost the bearer chose by not putting it down.

stages.name: Faint Ring
stages.description: A shard that will not stop softly ringing. Sharpens sonic work a little.
stages.narrationHints: A note under everything, just at the edge of hearing.

stages.name: Answering Ring
stages.description: The shard has begun to answer your casts before you finish them — stronger sonic work, and the first whispers.
stages.grant: Sonic casts sharpen further; the GM may offer the shard 'answering' a beat early.
stages.narrationHints: It rings back now, in a voice not quite yours.

stages.name: The Voice in the Stone
stages.description: The shard has learned to return voices, like the Choir it came from. Powerful — and it wants to be listened to. Putting it down is now its own decision.
stages.grant: Strong sonic amplification; once per scene the shard offers a true reading in a returned voice — at a cost the GM names.
stages.narrationHints: It speaks in the voices of the drowned. It has been waiting for someone to keep it this long.

items.substrateNote: Stone from the drowned tiers of a pre-Transition work. It came out of a pool and it kept some.
